title: Lovable: add microphone to metame.live iframe allow attr
body:
We shipped a voice-to-text mic affordance in the aigentMe compose
modals (Gmail, Doc, Calendar, Sheet, Slides, Marketa) and the
experience-guide setup textareas inside dev-beta.aigentz.me. The flow
uses MediaRecorder + getUserMedia + a Whisper transcription endpoint,
so it works in Brave, iOS Safari, Firefox — everywhere with mic access.

Right now it fails inside the metame.live thin-client shell because the
parent iframe does not delegate microphone permission. Browsers (Brave,
Firefox, iOS Safari) block getUserMedia in iframes unless the parent
declares it.

Please update the iframe element in the metame.live shell (the one that
loads https://dev-beta.aigentz.me/...) to add microphone to its allow
attribute:

  <iframe
    src=\"https://dev-beta.aigentz.me/...\"
    allow=\"microphone; clipboard-read; clipboard-write; fullscreen\"
    ...
  />

We have already opted dev-beta in by setting Permissions-Policy:
microphone=(self) on the embed responses, so once the parent delegates
via the allow attribute, getUserMedia will succeed.

No other changes needed on Lovable's side — the audio capture, upload,
transcription, and prompt-fill all live inside dev-beta.
